patientStory:
[REF2]40-year-old[/REF2] [REF3]male presenting for[/REF3] follow-up of dental and oral maxillofacial conditions. He carries diagnoses of [REF5]periapical abscess without sinus[/REF5], [REF6]traumatic fracture of tooth[/REF6], and [REF7]dental caries[/REF7], all with onset dates in late October 2023. He was recently prescribed [REF11]amoxicillin-clavulanate 875 mg twice daily[/REF11] and [REF12]chlorhexidine gluconate 0.12% solution 15 mL oromucosal twice daily[/REF12] on 10/27/2023 for management of his acute infectious process.

[REF26]On 11/01/2023, he presented for follow-up after incision and drainage of left maxillary buccal vestibular abscess performed on 10/27/2023.[/REF26] [REF27]Facial swelling had resolved, though[/REF27] [REF28]continued pain in the left upper quadrant of the mouth persisted.[/REF28] [REF20]Panoramic imaging revealed[/REF20] [REF21]a large mandibular radiolucent lesion (19x13 mm) to the right mandible[/REF21], [REF22]tooth #31 with large distal caries and associated periapical radiolucency[/REF22], [REF23]root tips #12, 13 with periapical radiolucency[/REF23], [REF24]root tips #15 with periapical radiolucency[/REF24], and [REF25]tooth #16 with mesial decay[/REF25]. [REF31]Extraction of root tips #12, 13, 15 and tooth #31 was performed[/REF31], and [REF32]the Penrose drain was removed.[/REF32] [REF35]Follow-up was scheduled for one week later.[/REF35]

items: [REF28]Continued pain in the left upper quadrant of the mouth persisted[/REF28]

PATIENT STORY ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[REF2]40-year-old[/REF2] [REF3]male presenting for[/REF3] follow-up of dental and oral maxillofacial conditions. He carries diagnoses of [REF5]periapical abscess without sinus[/REF5], [REF6]traumatic fracture of tooth[/REF6], and [REF7]dental caries[/REF7], all with onset dates in late October 2023. He was recently prescribed [REF11]amoxicillin-clavulanate 875 mg twice daily[/REF11] and [REF12]chlorhexidine gluconate 0.12% solution 15 mL oromucosal twice daily[/REF12] on 10/27/2023 for management of his acute infectious process.

[REF26]On 11/01/2023, he presented for follow-up after incision and drainage of left maxillary buccal vestibular abscess performed on 10/27/2023.[/REF26] [REF27]Facial swelling had resolved, though[/REF27] [REF28]continued pain in the left upper quadrant of the mouth persisted.[/REF28] [REF20]Panoramic imaging revealed[/REF20] [REF21]a large mandibular radiolucent lesion (19x13 mm) to the right mandible[/REF21], [REF22]tooth #31 with large distal caries and associated periapical radiolucency[/REF22], [REF23]root tips #12, 13 with periapical radiolucency[/REF23], [REF24]root tips #15 with periapical radiolucency[/REF24], and [REF25]tooth #16 with mesial decay[/REF25]. [REF31]Extraction of root tips #12, 13, 15 and tooth #31 was performed[/REF31], and [REF32]the Penrose drain was removed.[/REF32] [REF35]Follow-up was scheduled for one week later.[/REF35]
